questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $|x| < 1$, the coefficient of $x^3$ in the expansion of $\log \left(1+x+x^2\right)$ in ascending powers of $x$, is
questions.solution:
We have,
$\log \left(1+x+x^2\right)=\log \frac{\left(1-x^3\right)}{(1-x)}$
$\begin{aligned} & =\log \left(1-x^3\right)-\log (1-x) \\ & =-\left[x^3+\frac{x^6}{2}+\frac{x^9}{3}+\ldots\right]+\left[x+\frac{x^2}{2}+\frac{x^3}{3}+\ldots\right] \\ & \text { Coefficient of } x^3=-1+\frac{1}{3}=-\frac{2}{3}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\frac{x-4}{x^2-5 x+6}$ can be expanded in the ascending powers of $x$, then the coefficient of $x^3$ is
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{x-4}{x^2-5 x+6}=\frac{x-4}{(x-2)(x-3)} \\ & =\frac{2}{(x-2)}-\frac{1}{(x-3)}=2(x-2)^{-1}-(x-3)^{-1} \\ & =2(-2)^{-1}\left(1-\frac{x}{2}\right)^{-1}-(-3)^{-1}\left(1-\frac{x}{3}\right)^{-1} \\ & =-\left[1+\left(\frac{x}{2}\right)+\left(\frac{x}{2}\right)^2+\left(\frac{x}{2}\right)^3+\ldots\right] \\ & \quad+\frac{1}{3}\left[1+\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)+\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)^2+\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)^3+\ldots\right] \\ & \therefore \text { Coefficient of } x^3 \text { in } \frac{x-4}{x^2-5 x+6} .\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & =-\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^3+\frac{1}{3}\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^3 \\ & =-\frac{1}{8}+\frac{1}{81}=\frac{-81+8}{648} \\ & =-\frac{73}{648}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The coefficient of $x^k$ in the expansion of $\frac{1-2 x-x^2}{e^{-x}}$ is
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \text { Now, } \frac{1-2 x-x^2}{e^{-x}} \\ & =\left(1-2 x-x^2\right)\left(e^x\right) \\ & =\left(1-2 x-x^2\right)\left(1+x+\frac{x^2}{2 !}+\frac{x^3}{3 !}+\ldots\right. \\ & \left.+\frac{x^k}{k !}+\ldots \infty\right) \\ & =\left(1+x+\frac{x^2}{2 !}+\ldots+\frac{x^k}{k !}+\ldots \infty\right)-2\left(x+x^2+\frac{x^3}{2 !}\right. \\ & \left.+\ldots+\frac{x^k}{(k-1) !}+\frac{x^{k+1}}{k !}+\ldots \infty\right) \\ & -\left(x^2+x^3+\frac{x^4}{2 !}+\ldots .\right. \\ & \left.\frac{x^k}{(k-2) !}+\frac{x^{k+1}}{(k-1) !}+\frac{x^{k+2}}{k !}+\ldots \infty\right) \\ & \end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \therefore \text { Coefficient of } x^k \text { in }\left(\frac{1-2 x-x^2}{e^{-x}}\right) \\ &=\frac{1}{k !}-\frac{2}{(k-1) !}-\frac{1}{(k-2) !} \\ &=\frac{1}{k !}-\frac{2 k}{k(k-1) !}-\frac{k(k-1)}{k(k-1)(k-2) !} \\ &=\frac{1}{k !}-\frac{2 k}{k !}-\frac{k(k-1)}{k !} \\ &=\frac{1-k-k^2}{k !}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math><msup><mrow><mn>5</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>97</mn></mrow></msup></math> is divided by <math><mn>52</mn></math><math> <mo>,</mo></math> then the remainder obtained is
questions.solution:
We know that, 54=625=52×12+1

⇒54=52λ+1 , where λ is a positive integer.

⇒5424=52λ+124

= C02452λ24+C152λ23+C22452λ2224+…+C2352λ+C242424 (by binomial theorem)

⇒596=52C0245223λ24+C15223λ2224+…+C23λ24+1

=a multiple of 52+1

On multiplying both sides by 5 , we get

597=596⋅5=5 a multiple of 52+5

Hence, the required remainder is 5 .
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The coefficient of <math> <mrow> <msup> <mi>x</mi> <mi>n</mi> </msup> </mrow> </math> in the polynomial <math> <mrow> <mrow><mo>(</mo> <mrow> <mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><msup> <mtext> </mtext> <mrow> <mn>2</mn><mi>n</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow> </msup> <msub> <mi>C</mi> <mn>0</mn> </msub> </mrow> <mo>)</mo></mrow><mrow><mo>(</mo> <mrow> <mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><msup> <mtext> </mtext> <mrow> <mn>2</mn><mi>n</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow> </msup> <msub> <mi>C</mi> <mn>1</mn> </msub> </mrow> <mo>)</mo></mrow><mrow><mo>(</mo> <mrow> <mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><msup> <mtext> </mtext> <mrow> <mn>2</mn><mi>n</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow> </msup> <msub> <mi>C</mi> <mn>2</mn> </msub> </mrow> <mo>)</mo></mrow><mn>.....</mn><mrow><mo>(</mo> <mrow> <mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><msup> <mtext> </mtext> <mrow> <mn>2</mn><mi>n</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow> </msup> <msub> <mi>C</mi> <mi>n</mi> </msub> </mrow> <mo>)</mo></mrow></mrow> </math> is
questions.solution:
Given Expression is,  ( x+   2n+1 C 0 )( x+   2n+1 C 1 )( x+   2n+1 C 2 ).....( x+   2n+1 C n )

If P is coefficient of x n then,

P=   2n+1 C 0 +   2n+1 C 1 +   2n+1 C 2 +.....+   2n+1 C n  -------(1)

⇒P=   2n+1 C 2n+1 +   2n+1 C 2n +   2n+1 C 2n−1 +.....+   2n+1 C n+1 ------(2) ( ∵   n C r = n C n−r )

adding (1) and (2)

2P=( 2n+1 C 0 + 2n+1 C 1 +....... + 2n+1 C 2n+1 )

2P= 2 2n+1

∴   P= 2 2n
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If ${ }^{n-3} C_r+B^{n-3} C_{r-1}+B^{\prime n-3} C_{r-2}+{ }^{n-3} C_{r-3}$ $={ }^n C_r$ holds. for all $n \geq r \geq 3$, then $\left(B \cdot B^{\prime}\right)=$.
questions.solution:
${ }^{n-3} C_r+B^{n-3} C_{r-1}+B^{\prime n-3} C_{r-2}$
$+{ }^{n-3} C_{r-3}={ }^n C_r$
$\Rightarrow\left({ }^{n-3} C_r+{ }^{n-3} C_{r-1}\right)+\left({ }^{n-3} C_{r-1}+{ }^{n-3} C_{r-2}\right)$ $+\left({ }^{n-3} C_{r-2}+{ }^{n-3} C_{r-3}\right)$ $+(B-2){ }^{n-3} C_{r-1}+\left(B^{\prime}-2\right){ }^{n-3} C_{r-2}={ }^n C_3$
$\Rightarrow\left[{ }^{n-2} C_r+{ }^{n-2} C_{r-1}\right]+{ }^{n-2} C_{r-2}$ $+\left({ }^{n-3} C_{r-1}+{ }^{n-3} C_{r-2}\right)+(B-3){ }^{n-3} C_{r-1}$ $+\left(B^{\prime}-3\right){ }^{n-3} C_{r-2}={ }^n C_r$
$\Rightarrow{ }^{n-1} C_r+\left[{ }^{n-2} C_{r-2}+{ }^{n-2} C_{r-1}\right]$ $+(B-3)^{n-3} C_{r-2}+\left(B^{\prime}-3\right){ }^{n-3} C_{r-2}={ }^n C_r$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow{ }^n C_r+(B-3){ }^{n-3} C_{r-1}+\left(B^{\prime}-3\right)^{n-3} C_{r-2}={ }^n C_r \\ & \Rightarrow(B-3){ }^{n-3} C_{r-1}+\left(B^{\prime}-3\right){ }^{n-3} C_{r-2}=0\end{aligned}$
It is only possible when, $B-3=0$ and $B^{\prime}-3=0$ $\therefore\left(B, B^{\prime}\right) \equiv(3,3)$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.solution:
\(\left(1-x+x^2\right)^{10}=a_0+a_1 x+a_2 x^2+\ldots \ldots+a_{20} x^{20}\) On differentiating
\(\begin{aligned}
& 10\left(1-x+x^2\right)^9 \cdot(-1+2 x)=a_1+2 a_2 x+3 a_3 x^2 \\
& +\ldots \ldots+20 a_{20} x^{19}
\end{aligned}\)
At \(x=1\)
\(\Rightarrow \quad a_1+2 a_2+3 a_3+\ldots \ldots+20 a_{20}=10\)
At \(x=0 \Rightarrow a_1=-10\)
So, \(2 a_2+3 a_3+\ldots \ldots+20 a_{20}=20\)
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The numerically greatest term in the binomial expansion of $(2 a-3 b)^{19}$ when $a=\frac{1}{4}$ and $b=\frac{2}{3}$ is
questions.solution:
We have, $(2 a-3 b)^{19}=2^{19} \cdot a^{19}\left(1-\frac{3 b}{2 a}\right)^{19}$
Ne know that, the $r^{\text {th }}$ term of greatest term of
$$
\begin{aligned}
(1+x)^n & =\left[\frac{(n+1)|x|}{1+|x|}\right] \\
n & =19, x=-\frac{3 b}{2 a}
\end{aligned}
$$
Here, $\quad n=19, x=-\frac{3 b}{2 a}$
$$
r=\left[\frac{(20)\left|\frac{3 b}{2 a}\right|}{1+\frac{3 b}{2 a}}\right]=\left[\frac{20}{1+4} \times 4\right] \quad\left[\because b=\frac{2}{3}, a=\frac{1}{4}\right]
$$
$$
r=16
$$
greatest term of $(2 a-3 b)^{19}$ is
$$
\begin{aligned}
& ={ }^{19} C_{16} \times 2^{19} \cdot a^{19}\left(\frac{3 b}{2 a}\right)^{16} \\
& ={ }^{19} C_3 \times 2^{19} \times\left(\frac{1}{4}\right)^{19} \times(4)^{16} \quad\left[\because{ }^{19} C_{16}={ }^{19} C_3\right] \\
& ={ }^{19} C_3 \times 2^{19} \times \frac{1}{2^{38}} \times 2^{32}={ }^{19} C_3 \times 2^{13}
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.q: If the middle term in the binomial expansion of <math><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></mrow></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>10</mn></mrow></msup></math> is <math><mfrac><mrow><mn>63</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>8</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo>,</mo></math> then the value of <math><mn>6</mn><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></math> is
questions.solution:
Here, n=10, which is even.
Middle term =102+1th term =6th term
T 6 =   10 C 5 1 x 5 (xsinx) 5
⇒638=252sinx5
⇒sinx5=132
⇒sinx5=125
⇒sinx=12
⇒2sinx-1=0
⇒6sin2x+sinx-2=2sinx-13sinx+2=0
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: The sixth term in the expansion of $\left(3-\sqrt{\frac{17}{4}+3 \sqrt{2}}\right)^{10}$ is a
questions.solution:
We have,
$$
\left(3-\sqrt{\frac{17}{4}+3 \sqrt{2}}\right)^{20} \Rightarrow T_6=-{ }^{20} C_5(3)^5\left(\frac{17}{4}+3 \sqrt{2}\right)^{5 / 2}
$$
$\therefore \mathrm{r}_6=$ negative irrational number

questions.q: The number of terms in the expansion of <math><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msup><mrow><mn>5</mn></mrow><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>6</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mn>7</mn></mrow><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>9</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>1824</mn></mrow></msup></math> which are integers is
questions.solution:
TK+1=1824CK 51824-K6 7K9

Where, K=0,1,2.....,1824
Now, 1824-K6 will be an integer if
K=0,6,12,18,....,1824

Also, K9 will be an integer if K=0,9,18,.....,1818.
⇒ Both are integers if K=0,18,36,.....,1818

If the number of terms =n,

⇒1818=0+n–118⇒n=102

questions.q: The number of rational terms in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><msup><mn>3</mn><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>4</mn></mfrac></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mn>7</mn><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>6</mn></mfrac></msup></mrow></mfenced><mn>144</mn></msup></math> is
questions.solution:
Let,

S=314+716144

General term of S is given by

Tr+1=Cr144 3144-r47r6

Now, for the rational terms

144-r4∈Wwhole number

For this r must be the multiple of 4 therefore

r=0, 4, 8, ..., 140, 144

And,

r6∈Wwhole number

For this r must be the multiple of 6 therefore

r=0, 6, 12, 18, 24,  ....., 138, 144

Now, common terms are

0, 12, 24, ...., 144

This is an AP. So,

144=0+n-112

⇒n=13.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
The sum of all the coefficients in the binomial expansion of \((1+2 x)^n\) is 6561. Let \(R=(I+2 x)^n=I+F\), where \(I \in N\) and \(0  < F < \mathrm{l}\).
If \(x=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\), then \(1-\frac{F}{1+(\sqrt{2}-1)^4}=\)
questions.solution:
It is given that sum of all the coefficients in the binomial expansion of \((1+2 x)^n\) is \(6561=(1+2)^n\) on putting \(x=1\).
\(\begin{array}{ll}
\Rightarrow & 3^n=6561 \\
\Rightarrow & n=8
\end{array}\)
Now, at \(x=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\), then \(R=(1+2 x)^n=I+F\)
\(\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow R=(\sqrt{2}+1)^8=I+F, \text { where } I \in N \text { and } 0  < F < 1 \\
& \therefore(\sqrt{2}-1)^8=F^{\prime}, \text { where } 0 < F^{\prime} < 1 \\
& \therefore(\sqrt{2}+1)^8+(\sqrt{2}-1)^8=I+\left(F+F^{\prime}\right) \\
& \Rightarrow 2\left[(\sqrt{2})^8+{ }^8 C_2(\sqrt{2})^6+{ }^8 C_4(\sqrt{2})^4+{ }^8 C_6(\sqrt{2})^2+{ }^8 C_8\right] \\
& \quad=I+\left(F+F^{\prime}\right) \\
& \Rightarrow \text { Even integer }=I+\left(F+F^{\prime}\right) \\
& \Rightarrow F+F^{\prime} \in \text { Integer } \\
& \because 0 < F < 1 \text { and } 0 < F^{\prime} < 1 \Rightarrow 0 < F+F^{\prime} < 2 \\
& \text { So, } F+F^{\prime}=1 \\
& \Rightarrow F=1-F^{\prime}=1-(\sqrt{2}-1)^8
\end{aligned}\)
\(\begin{aligned}
& \text { So, } 1-\frac{F}{1+(\sqrt{2}-1)^4}=1-\frac{1-(\sqrt{2}-1)^8}{1+(\sqrt{2}-1)^4} \\
& =1-\frac{\left[1+(\sqrt{2}-1)^4\right]\left[1-(\sqrt{2}-1)^4\right]}{1+(\sqrt{2}-1)^4} \\
& =1-\left[1-(\sqrt{2}-1)^4\right]=(\sqrt{2}-1)^4
\end{aligned}\)
Hence, option (3) is correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: The first negative coefficient in the terms occurring in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi><msup><mo>)</mo><mfrac><mn>21</mn><mn>5</mn></mfrac></msup></math> is
questions.solution:
The expansion of  1+x215=1+215x+215215-12!x2+215215-1215-23!x3+…

Simplify the above expansion.

1+x215=1+215x+21×1652×2!x2+21×16×1153×3!x3+21×16×11×654×4!+..

The first negative coefficient in the terms occurring in the expansion of (1+x)215 is

=-21×16×11×6×1×456×6!

=-8870457×144

=-61657

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The least value of $n$ such that ${ }^{(n-1)} C_6+{ }^{(n-1)} C_7 < { }^n C_8$ is
questions.solution:
${ }^{(n-1)} C_6+{ }^{(n-1)} C_7 < { }^n C_8$
$\Rightarrow{ }^n C_7 < { }^n C_8$
$\Rightarrow{ }^n C_7-{ }^n C_8 < 0$
$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{n !}{7 !(n-7) !}-\frac{n !}{8 !(n-8) !} < 0$
$\Rightarrow \frac{n !}{7 !(n-8) !}\left[\frac{1}{n-7}-\frac{1}{8}\right] < 0$
$\Rightarrow \frac{8-(n-7)}{8(n-7)} < 0$
$\Rightarrow 15-n < 0 \Rightarrow n>15$
$\therefore \quad$ The least $n$ satisfying above condition is $n=16$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: When <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>|</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>|</mo><mo><</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo>,</mo></math> the coefficient of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>4</mn></msup></math> in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mn>3</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>5</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>(</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>)</mo></mrow></mfrac></math> is
questions.solution:
Rewrite the given expression

F=-x2-53x+1(1-x)-1(1+2x)-11+x3-1

=-x2-53x+11+x+x2+x3+x41-2x+4x2-8x3+16x41-x3+x29-x327+x481

On neglecting higher degree terms

=-1+x+x2+x3+x4-53x+53x2+53x3+53x4+x2+x3+x4

Simplify the above expression.

=-1-23x+x23+x33+x431-7x3+439x2-25927x3+155581x4

=-216681

=-72227

questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>L</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>M</mi></math> are respectively the coefficient of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>7</mn></mrow></msup></math> in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>a</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mi>b</mi><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mn>11</mn></msup></math> and the coefficient of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>7</mn></msup></math> in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>b</mi><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mi>a</mi><mi>x</mi></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mn>11</mn></msup></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>L</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>M</mi><mo>=</mo></math>
questions.solution:
General term of a+bx2 is

Tr+1=Cr11ax11-r×bx2r

⇒Tr+1=Cr11 a11-r·brx11-3r

For coefficient of x-7,

11-3r=-7

⇒ 3r=18

⇒  r=6

So, 

T6+1=C611 a11-6 b6  x-7

Tr+1=C611 a5b6x-7

So, L=C611a5b6

Similarly, general term of bx2+ax11 is

Tr+1=Cr11 bx211-r×axr

⇒Tr+1=Cr11 b11-r×ar×x22-3r

So, for coefficient of x7

 22-3r=7 ⇒r=5

Hence,

M=11C5b6a5

So, L+M=2×C611a5b6=924a5b6

And, general term of ax2+bx12 is

Tr+1=Cr12ax212-rbxr

Tr+1=Cr12 a12-r br x24-3r

For coeffiicent of x6, 24-3r=6  ⇒ r=6

So, coefficient of x6 is

=C612 a6 b6=924 a6b6

Hence,

L+M=1a×[Coefficient of x6 in ax2+bx12]
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
If $\mathrm{k}$ is the coefficient of $x^5$ in the expansion of
$\left(2 x^2-\frac{1}{3 x^3}\right)^5$ then $\frac{3 \mathrm{k}}{2}=$
questions.solution:
Given expression $\left(2 x^2-\frac{1}{3 x^3}\right)^5$.
General term $\mathrm{T}_{r+1}={ }^5 \mathrm{C}_r\left(2 x^2\right)^{5-r}\left(\frac{-1}{3 x^3}\right)^r$
$\mathrm{T}_{r+1}={ }^5 \mathrm{C}_r(2)^{5-r}\left(\frac{-1}{3}\right)^r x^{10-2 r-3 r}$
Here, $10-2 r-3 r$ should be equal to 5 .
Then, $10-2 r-3 r=5$
$\begin{aligned}
& 5=5 r \\
& r=1
\end{aligned}$
Now, $\mathrm{T}_2={ }^5 \mathrm{C}_1(2)^{5-1}\left(\frac{-1}{3}\right)^1 x^{10-2-3}$
$=\frac{5 \times 4 !}{4 !} \times 16 \times \frac{-1}{3} \times x^5$
So, $\mathrm{K}=5 \times 16 \times \frac{-1}{3}=\frac{-80}{3}$
Here, $\frac{3 \mathrm{~K}}{2}=\frac{3}{2} \times \frac{-80}{3}=-40$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: For <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>N</mi></math>, in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mroot><msup><mi>x</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mn>4</mn></mroot><mo>+</mo><mi>a</mi><mroot><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>5</mn></msup><mn>4</mn></mroot></mrow></mfenced><mi>n</mi></msup></math>, the sum of all binomial coefficients lies between <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>200</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>400</mn></math> and the term independent of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>448</mn></math>. Then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi></math> is
questions.solution:
Given that,

x-34+ax54n

General Term Tr+1= nCr(1x34)n-rar(x54)r

=nCrarx-34n+34r+54r

For independent of x-

r=n3434+54

=3n484

∴ r=3n8

The term independent of x is 448.

⇒ C3n8na3n8=448

Sum of coefficient between 200 and 400 is given as

200<1+an<400

According to the question,
nCrar = 448
⇒ 8C3a3=448
⇒ 56a3=448
⇒ a3=8 
⇒ a = 2

⇒n=8, a=2

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If the ratio of the 7 th term from the beginning to the 7 th term from the end in the expansion of $\left(\sqrt[3]{2}+\frac{1}{\sqrt[3]{3}}\right)^n$ is $\frac{1}{6}$, then $n=$
questions.solution:
Since, the general term in the expansion of
$\left(\sqrt[3]{2}+\frac{1}{\sqrt[3]{3}}\right)^n$ is
$T_{r+1}={ }^n C_r\left(2^{1 / 3}\right)^{n-r}\left(\frac{1}{3^{1 / 3}}\right)^r$
Now,
the 7 th term from beginning $={ }^n C_6 2^{\frac{n-6}{3}} 3^{-2}$
and the 7 th term from end $={ }^n C_6\left(\frac{1}{3^{1 / 3}}\right)^{n-6}\left(2^{1 / 3}\right)^6$
$\because$ It is given that,
$\frac{{ }^n C_6 2^{\frac{n-6}{3}} 3^{-2}}{{ }^n C_6 3^{\frac{6-n}{3}} 2^2}=\frac{1}{6} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{6^{\frac{6-n}{3}+2}}=\frac{1}{6}$
$\begin{array}{ll}\Rightarrow & \frac{6-n}{3}+2=1 \Rightarrow \frac{6-n}{3}=-1 \\ \Rightarrow & 6-n=-3 \Rightarrow n=9\end{array}$
Hence, option (c) is correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: For $|x| < \frac{1}{2}$, if the coefficient of $x^{10}$ and the constant term in the expansion of $\frac{2 x^3+8 x^2-2 x-2}{(1-x)(1+x)(1-2 x)}$ in powers of $x$ are $l$ and $m$ respectively, then $1 m=$
questions.solution:
The given expression is
$\frac{2 x^3+8 x^2-2 x-2}{(1-x)(1+x)(1-2 x)}=\frac{2 x^3+8 x^2-2 x-2}{(2 x-1)(x-1)(x+1)}$
$=1+\frac{A}{2 x-1}+\frac{B}{x-1}+\frac{C}{x+1}$
(By partial fraction)
$\begin{aligned} \Rightarrow 2 x^3+8 x^2-2 x & -2 \\ =\left(2 x^3\right. & \left.-x^2-2 x+1\right)+A\left(x^2-1\right) \\ & +B\left(2 x^2+x-1\right)+C\left(2 x^2-3 x+1\right)\end{aligned}$
On comparing, we get
$\begin{aligned}-1+A+2 B+2 C & =8 \\ -2+B-3 C & =-2\end{aligned}$
and $\quad 1-A-B+C=-2$
On solving we have $A=1, B=3$ and $C=1$
$\therefore$ Given expression is
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{2 x^3+8 x^2-2 x-2}{(1-x)(1+x)(1-2 x)}=1-\frac{1}{1-2 x}-\frac{3}{1-x}+\frac{1}{1+x} \\ = & 1-(1-2 x)^{-1}-3(1-x)^{-1}+(1+x)^{-1}\end{aligned}$
$\therefore$ Coefficient of $x^{10}$ is
$-2^{10}-3+1=-2-2^{10}=l$
and constant term is $1-1-3+1=-2=m$
$\therefore \quad l m=4\left(1+2^9\right)$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $p$ is an integral multiple of 4 lying in between the coefficients of $x^4$ and $x$ in the expansion of $\left(x^2+\frac{1}{x}\right)^8$, then the number of such values of $p$ is
questions.solution:
The general term in the expansion of
$\left(x^2+\frac{1}{x}\right)^8$ is
$T_{r+1}={ }^8 C_r\left(x^2\right)^{8-r}\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)^r={ }^8 C_r x^{16-3 r}$
For the coefficient of $x^4$, put $r=4$, we get
${ }^8 C_4=\frac{8 \times 7 \times 6 \times 5}{4 \times 3 \times 2}=70$
For the coefficient of $x$, put $r=5$, we get
${ }^8 C_5=\frac{8 \times 7 \times 6}{3 \times 2}=56$
Now, the numbers which are integral multiple of 4 and lying in between 56 and 70 are 60,64 and 68 . Hence, option (a) is correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The greatest term in the expansion of $(1+x)^{15}$, when $x=\frac{1}{2}$ is
questions.solution:
Let $T_{r+1}$ and $T_r$ denotes the $(\mathrm{r}+1)$ th and $r$ th term in the expansion of $(1+x)^{15}$
$$
\therefore \quad T_{r+1}={ }^n C_r x^r \text { and } T_r={ }^n C_{r-1} x^{r-1} \text {, where } n=15
$$

For greatest term in the expansion
$$
\begin{aligned}
& T_{r+1} \geq T_r \Rightarrow{ }^n C_r x^r \geq{ }^n C_{r-1} x^{r-1} \\
& \Rightarrow \frac{n ! \cdot x^r}{r^{\prime} !(n-r) !} \geq \frac{n ! \cdot x^{r-1}}{(r-1) !(n-r+1) !}
\end{aligned}
$$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow \quad \frac{x}{r \cdot(r-1) !(\mathrm{n}-\mathrm{r}) !} \geq \frac{1}{(r-1) ! \cdot(n-r+1) \cdot(n-r) !} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \frac{x}{r} \geq \frac{1}{(n-r+1)} \\
& \Rightarrow \frac{1}{r}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right) \geq \frac{1}{(16-r)} \quad\left(\because n=15 \text { and } x=\frac{1}{2}\right) \\
& \Rightarrow 16-r \geq 2 r \\
& \Rightarrow 3 r \leq 16 \Rightarrow r \leq \frac{16}{3}
\end{aligned}
$$

Hence $r=5$ will be the greatest term
$$
\therefore \quad T_{r+1}=T_{5+1}={ }^{15} C_5 x^5={ }^{15} C_5\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^5=\frac{1}{32}{ }^{15} C_5
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: The nineth term in the expansion of <math> <mrow> <msup> <mrow> <mfenced close="}" open="{" separators=","> <mrow> <msup> <mn>3</mn> <mrow> <msub> <mtext>log</mtext> <mn>3</mn> </msub> <msqrt> <mn>2</mn> <msup> <mn>5</mn> <mrow> <mtext>x</mtext> <mo>-</mo> <mn>1</mn> </mrow> </msup> <mo>+</mo> <mn>7</mn> </msqrt> </mrow> </msup> <mo>+</mo> <msup> <mn>3</mn> <mrow> <mo>-</mo> <mn>1</mn> <mo>/</mo> <mn>8</mn> <msub> <mtext>log</mtext> <mn>3</mn> </msub> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <msup> <mn>5</mn> <mrow> <mtext>x</mtext> <mo>-</mo> <mn>1</mn> </mrow> </msup> <mo>+</mo> <mn>1</mn> </mrow> </mfenced> </mrow> </msup> </mrow> </mfenced> </mrow> <mrow> <mn>1</mn> <mn>0</mn> </mrow> </msup> </mrow> </math>  is equal to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>180</mn></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is -
questions.solution:
We have,

3 log 3 2 5 x - 1 + 7 + 3 - 1 / 8  log 3 5 x - 1 + 1 1 0

= 2 5 x - 1 + 7 + 5 x - 1 + 1 - 1 / 8 1 0           ∵  a log a  N = N

Here, T9 = 180,

⇒    1 0 C 8 2 5 x - 1 + 7 1 0 - 8 5 x + 1 + 1 - 1 / 8 8 = 1 8 0

⇒    1 0 C 8 2 5 x - 1 + 7 5 x + 1 + 1 - 1 = 1 8 0

⇒    4 5 2 5 x + 1 + 7 5 x - 1 + 1 = 1 8 0

⇒    2 5 x - 1 + 7 5 x - 1 + 1 = 4

⇒    y 2 + 7 y + 1 = 4 ,   where,  y = 5 x - 1

⇒    y 2 - 4 y + 3 = 0

⇒    y = 3 ,  1

⇒    5 x - 1 = 3  or  5 x - 1 = 1

⇒    5 x = 1 5  or  5 x = 5

⇒    x = log 5 1 5  or  x = 1

Hence (ii) is correct answer.

C0n+C1nλ+C2nλ2+…+Cnnλn×C0nμn+C1nμn-1+…+Cnn×C0nλn+C1nλn-1μ+…+Cnnμn

C03+C13+C23+…+Cn3=∑r=0nCrn3

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The greatest integer which divides the number <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>101</mn><mn>100</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></math> is
questions.solution:
For the greatest integer which divides the number 101100 - 1, consider

1 + x n = 1 + nx + n n - 1 2 x 2 + ........ + x n

                            {by Binomial theorem)

⇒ 1 + x n - 1 = nx + n - 1 2 x 2 + ........ + x n

If x = n, then;

1 + n n - 1 = n 2 + n n - 1 2 n 2 + ... + n n

⇒ 1 + n n - 1 = n 2 1 + n n - 1 2 + ... n n - 2

Put n=100 in the equation,

1 + 1 0 0 1 0 0 - 1 = 1 0 0 2 1 + 1 0 0 1 0 0 - 1 2 + ........ + 1 0 0 9 8

⇒101100-1=10021+100×992+...+10098⇒101100-1=1002m

Clearly, 101100 - 1 is divisible by 1002.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi></math> is an even positive integer greater than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn></math>, then the condition that the greatest term in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi></msup></math> may have the greatest coefficient also is
questions.solution:
If n is even, the greatest coefficient is nCn/2

∴    The greatest term  = nCn/2 xn/2

∴ nCn/2 xn/2> nCn/2−1xn/2−1

and nCn/2 xn/2> nCn/2+1xn/2+1

⇒                  n-n2+1n2x>1

and              n-n2+1+1n2+1x<1

⇒n/2n/2+1<x<n2+1n2

Hence, nn+2<x<n+2n
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: A possible value of ' <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> ', for which the ninth term in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced open="{" close="}"><mrow><msup><mn>3</mn><mrow><msub><mi>log</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><msqrt><msup><mn>25</mn><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>7</mn></msqrt></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mn>3</mn><mrow><mfenced><mrow><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>8</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><msub><mi>log</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mfenced><mrow><msup><mn>5</mn><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced><mn>10</mn></msup></math> in the increasing powers of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>3</mn><mrow><mfenced><mrow><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>8</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><msub><mi>log</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mfenced><mrow><msup><mn>5</mn><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></mrow></msup></math> is equal to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>180</mn></math>, is :
questions.solution:
3log325x-1+7+3-18log35x-1+110

=25x-1+7+5x-1+1-1810, using anlogax=xn

Using the formula of the general term in the Binomial theorem for non negative integral index, i.e., Tr+1=Crnan-rbr  in a+bn, we get, 

C81025(x-1)+7×5(x-1)+1-1=180

⇒25x-1+75(x-1)+1=4

⇒t2+7t+1=4, taking t=5x-1

⇒t=1,3⇒5x-1=1⇒5x-1=50

⇒x-1=0 (one of the possible value).

⇒x=1

questions.q: If <math><mn>1</mn><msup><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>99</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><msup><mrow><mn>9</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>93</mn></mrow></msup></math> is divided by <math><mn>162</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then the remainder is
questions.solution:
1399-1993= odd number – odd number = even number
⇒ It is divisible by 2.
Also, 1399=1+1299=1+99C1⋅12+99C2⋅122+99C3⋅123+99C4⋅124+....
=1+99×12+99×982⋅122+3399×4998×973×2×123+81Ι1
=1+99×12+99×49⋅122+3×11×49×97×123+81Ι1
=1+99×12+8111×49×16+11×49×97×64+Ι1
1399=1+99×12+81Ι2
1993=1+1893=1+93C1⋅18+93C2⋅182+....
=1+93×18+81Ι3
⇒1399-1993=99×12-93×18+81Ι2-Ι3
=2711×4-31×2+81Ι4
=2744-62+81Ι4=27×-18+81Ι4
=81-6+Ι4=81Ι5
Hence, it is also divisible by 81.
So, it is divisible by 162⇒ Remainder =0.

questions.q: If <math><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>6</mn></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msub><mrow><mi>a</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><msub><mrow><mi>a</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>+</mo><msub><mrow><mi>a</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>12</mn></mrow></msub><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>12</mn></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo></math> then the value of <math><msub><mrow><mi>a</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mo>+</mo><msub><mrow><mi>a</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msub><mo>+</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>+</mo><msub><mrow><mi>a</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>12</mn></mrow></msub></math> is
questions.solution:
Given , (1+x-2x2)6=1+a1x+a2x2+...+a12x12 ...(i)

On putting x=1 in Equation (i),

we get 1+1-26=1+a1+a2+…+a12

⟹(0)6=1+a1+a2+...+a12             ....(ii)

On putting x=-1 in Equation (i),

we get 1-1-26=1-a1+a2-a3+…+a12

⟹(-2)6=1-a1+a2-a3+...+a12 ...(iii)

On adding Equations (ii) and (iii) we get

-26=21+a2+a4+…+a12

⟹642-1=a2+a4+…+a12

∴a2+a4+…+a12=31

questions.q: If the <math><msup><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">h</mi></mrow></msup><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi></math> term in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msqrt><msup><mi>x</mi><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><msub><mi>log</mi><mn>10</mn></msub><mo>⁡</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfrac></msup></msqrt><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>12</mn></mfrac></msup></mrow></mfenced><mn>6</mn></msup></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>200</mn></math>, then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is (where, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>></mo><mn>1</mn></math>)
questions.solution:
T4=C36x1log10x+13x1123

⇒200=20x1log10x+132x14
⇒10=x321log10x+1+14

⇒1t=321t+1+14 ….. [Take t=log10x]
⇒t2+3t-4=0
⇒t-1t+4=0
⇒t=1 or t=-4
⇒log10x=1 or log10x=-4
⇒x=10 … [as x>1]

questions.q: If only the <math><msup><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">h</mi></mrow></msup></math> term in the expansion of <math><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>8</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>10</mn></mrow></msup></math> has the greatest numerical value, then the integral values of <math><mi>x</mi></math> are
questions.solution:
T3<T4>T5
⇒T4T3>1 and T5T4<1
Tr+1=Cr10210-r3x8r
T 4 T 3 = C 10 3 2 7 3x 8 3 C 10 2 2 8 3x 8 2 = x 2
T 5 T 4 = C 10 4 2 6 3x 8 4 C 10 3 2 7 3x 8 3 = 21 64 x
x2>1⇒x>2 or x<-2
2164x<1⇒-6421<x<6421
Taking intersection, we get,

 x∈-6421,-2∪2,6421

Hence, integral values of x=-3,+3

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Consider the following statements
I : The number of non-trivial even divisors of the number \(2^{\alpha_1} 3^{\alpha_2} 4^{\alpha_3} 5^{\alpha_4} 6^{\alpha_5}\), is \(\left(\alpha_1+1\right)\left(\alpha_2+1\right)\left(\alpha_3+1\right)\left(\alpha_4+1\right)\left(\alpha_5+1\right)-2\)
II : The number of non-trivial odd divisors of the number \(2^{\alpha_1} \cdot 3^{\alpha_2} \cdot 4^{\alpha_3} \cdot 5^{\alpha_4} \cdot 6^{\alpha_5}\), is \(\alpha_2+\alpha_4+\alpha_5+\alpha_2 \alpha_4+\alpha_4 \alpha_5\). Then
options: I is false and II is false
I is true and II is true
I is false and II is true
I is true and II is false
questions.solution:
Given number \(=2^{\alpha_1} 3^{\alpha_2} 4^{\alpha_3} 5^{\alpha_4} 6^{\alpha_5}\)
\(=2^{\alpha_1+2 \alpha_3+\alpha_5 3^{\alpha_2}+\alpha_5 5^{\alpha_4}}\)
Number of non-trivial even divisors
\(=\left(\alpha_1+2 \alpha_3+\alpha_5\right)\left(\alpha_2+\alpha_5+1\right)\left(\alpha_4+1\right)-1\)
And, number of non-trivial odd divisors
\(\begin{aligned}
& =\left(\alpha_2+\alpha_5+1\right)\left(\alpha_4+1\right)-1 \\
& =\alpha_2+\alpha_4+\alpha_5+\alpha_2 \alpha_4+\alpha_4 \alpha_5+1-1
\end{aligned}\)
\(\therefore\) Statement \(\mathrm{I}\) is false and \(\mathrm{II}\) is true.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The coefficients of \(x^{50}\) in \((1+x)^{101}\) \(\left(1-x+x^2\right)^{100}\) is......
questions.solution:
Given expression is
\(\begin{aligned}
& (1+x)^{101}\left(1-x+x^2\right)^{100} \\
& =(1+x)\left[(1+x)\left(1-x+x^2\right)\right]^{100} \\
& =(1+x)\left(1+x^3\right)^{100}
\end{aligned}\)
So, coefficient of \(x^{50}\) in \((1+x)^{101}\left(1-x+x^2\right)^{100}\) \(=\) coefficient of \(x^{50}\) in \((1+x)\left(1+x^3\right)^{100}\)
\(=\) coefficient of \(x^{50}\) in \(\left(1+x^3\right)^{100}\)
+ coefficient of \(x^{49}\) in \(\left(1+x^3\right)^{100}\)
\(\because\) In the expansion of \(\left(1+x^3\right)^{100}\), the power of \(x\) is multiple of 3 , and while 50 and 49 are not multiple of 3.
So, coefficient of \(x^{50}\) and 49 in the expansion of \(\left(1+x^3\right)^{100}\) is zero.
\(\therefore\) Coefficient of \(x^{50}\) in \((1+x)^{100}\left(1-x+x^2\right)^{101}\) is zero.
Hence, option (c) is correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>[</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>]</mo></math> represents the greatest integer not greater than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="]" open="["><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>100000</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mn>100000</mn></msup></mfenced><mo>=</mo></math>
questions.solution:
Let A=[(1+110000)10000]=[(1+0.0001)1000]

We know that,

(1+x)n=C0n+C1n·x+C2n·x2+Cnn·xnA=[C01000+C11000·(0.0001)+C21000·(0.0001)2+......+C10001000·(0.0001)n]A=[1+10000×0.0001+10000×9992×10-8+........]A=[1+1+0.49+...]A=[2.049+...]A=2.

questions.q: If $\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{n})=\mathrm{n} !(31-\mathrm{n}) !$, where $\mathrm{n} \in\{0,1,2, \ldots, 31\}$, then the minimum value of $f(n)$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text {} \because f(n)=n !(31-n) ! \\ & \text { So, } f(31-n)=(31-n !)(n !)=f(n) \Rightarrow f(0)=f(31) \\ & f(1)=f(30) \ldots \\ & \text { Also } f(0)>f(1)>f(2)>\ldots>f(15) < f(16) < f(17) \ldots \\ & < f(30) < f(31) \\ & \therefore f(15) \text { is minimum. Then, the minimum value is: } \\ & \Rightarrow f(15 !)=15 !(31-15) !=(15 !)(16 !)\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Let $x \in \mathbf{R}$ be so small that the powers of $x$ beyond two are insignificant and negligibly small. For such $x$, if $(1-x)^3(2+x)^6$ is approximated by $a+b x+c x^2$, then $a+b+c=$
questions.solution:
We have,
$\begin{gathered}
(1-x)^3=1-3 x+3 x^2 \text { [neglecting higher term] } \\
\begin{array}{c}
(2+x)^6={ }^6 C_0 2^6 x^0+{ }^6 C_1 2^5 x^1+{ }^6 C_2 2^4 x^2 \\
\text { [neglecting higher term] }
\end{array} \\
=64+192 x+240 x^2
\end{gathered}$
[neglecting higher term]
$=64+192 x+240 x^2$
Now,
$\begin{aligned}
& \begin{aligned}
&(1-x)^3(2+x)^6=\left(1-3 x+3 x^2\right)\left(64+192 x+240 x^2\right) \\
&= 64+192 x+240 x^2-192 x-576 x^2+192 x^2 \\
& \quad \text { (neglecting higher term) } \\
&=-144 x^2+64 \\
& \therefore \quad a=64, b=0, c=-144 \\
& \therefore a+b+c=64+0-144=-80
\end{aligned}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
